title: Fix dashboard data loading issue

requirements.text: Fix the dashboard page so that habit progress bars, today's stats cards, and activity timeline all load and display data correctly without remaining blank or failing to fetch.

quotes: fix the dashboard it couldnt load the data

acceptanceCriteria: Dashboard page renders without a blank or empty state when the user is authenticated
Habit progress bars display current vs. goal hours for today
Today's stats cards (total hours, earnings, etc.) show correct values
Activity timeline displays today's logged activities
No console errors related to data fetching or query failures on the dashboard

requirements.text: Ensure the React Query hooks used by DashboardPage (habits query, activities query, user profile query) correctly call the backend actor and handle loading/error states gracefully, showing a spinner or fallback UI instead of a blank screen while data is being fetched.

quotes: fix the dashboard it couldnt load the data

acceptanceCriteria: A loading indicator is shown while queries are in flight
An error message is shown if a query fails instead of a blank screen
Queries are not triggered before the backend actor is initialized
Stale or undefined actor references do not cause silent data fetch failures

requirements.text: Verify and fix the useActor hook integration in the dashboard context so the actor is fully initialized before any dashboard queries execute, preventing null/undefined actor calls that silently return no data.

quotes: the dashboard it couldnt load the data

acceptanceCriteria: Dashboard queries are enabled only when the actor is non-null and identity is authenticated
Re-authentication or page refresh correctly re-initializes the actor and reloads dashboard data

constraints: Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui
Do not change the backend data schema or Motoko actor logic unless required to fix a broken query endpoint
nonGoals: Adding new dashboard features or widgets
Changing the overall design or color theme
Modifying the habits, activities, or analytics pages
